title: Quantized orbital and spin Hall transport: interplay between $sp$-hybridization, altermagnetism and spin-orbit coupling
authors: Saikat Saha, Banasree Sadhukhan, Tanay Nag
abstract: We here explore the emergence of orbital and spin Hall effects, originating beyond the $L$-$S$ coupling, and investigate the interplay between inter-orbit hybridization, relativistic Rashba spin-orbit coupling (SOC), and non-relativistic SOC, namely altermagnetic (AM) order, in a two-dimensional model Hamiltonian. The orbital (spin) Hall responses are remarkably found to be quantized within a window of Fermi energy when the strength of AM order (Rashba SOC) exceeds (falls below) the scale set by $sp$-hybridization. Importantly, orbital and spin Hall quantizations are independent of Rashba SOC and AM order, respectively, while the uniform profiles of finite orbital and vanishingly small spin moments of bands around the Fermi energy. The microscopic origin of such quantization comes from the Fermi surface-activated orbital and spin Berry curvatures. The extent of the quantized regime is strongly controlled by the intra-orbital coupling strength. As the temperature increases, the quantization is significantly compromised in the spin Hall case. We extend our analysis to the orbital and spin Nernst coefficients where the pronounced dip-peak structures signal the existence of the quantization leading to experimental relevance.
summary: This theoretical work explores quantized orbital and spin Hall transport in 2D materials by modeling the interplay between $sp$-hybridization, altermagnetism, and SOC. The key finding is that the Hall responses become quantized over a specific Fermi energy window when magnetic order is tuned relative to hybridization scales. This quantization is microscopically rooted in the Berry curvature of the occupied electronic states.
main_problem: The paper investigates the emergence and quantization of orbital and spin Hall effects in systems beyond standard L-S coupling, focusing on the interplay between $sp$-hybridization, altermagnetism (AM) order, and spin-orbit coupling (SOC).
main_result: Quantized orbital and spin Hall responses are found when the AM order strength is tuned relative to the $sp$-hybridization scale, with the quantization being robustly linked to Fermi surface-activated Berry curvatures.
method: The study employs theoretical calculations using a two-dimensional model Hamiltonian to compute orbital and spin Berry curvatures and corresponding Hall/Nernst conductivities.
model_or_system: The model is a 2D Hamiltonian incorporating $sp$-hybridization, Rashba SOC ($\lambda_R$), and AM order ($J_A$) in a basis set spanning orbital and spin degrees of freedom.
key_observables: Orbital Hall (OH) and Spin Hall (SH) conductivities ($\sigma_{OH}, \sigma_{SH}$), and Orbital/Spin Nernst coefficients ($\sigma_{ON}, \sigma_{SN}$).
important_parameters: AM order strength ($J_A$), Rashba SOC ($\lambda_R$), $sp$-hybridization strength ($\gamma$), and Fermi energy ($E_F$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on a two-dimensional model Hamiltonian and assumes the quantization mechanism is governed by Fermi surface-activated Berry curvatures.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate band structures showing angular momentum distributions, and plots show the quantized plateau structures of OH and SH conductivities as functions of Fermi energy and various coupling strengths.
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes the Hamiltonian, calculates the relevant Berry curvatures, and then computes the transport coefficients ($\sigma_{OH}, \sigma_{SH}, \sigma_{ON}, \sigma_{SN}$) across different parameter regimes to demonstrate the quantization.
why_interesting: The focus on topological transport phenomena (Hall effects) driven by orbital and spin degrees of freedom, and the explicit modeling of hybridization and magnetic order, connects to modern condensed matter physics research areas.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Band Structure (a) with the distribution of ⟨Lz⟩and (b) with the distribution of ⟨Sz⟩, computed from the model Hamiltonian H. The inset plot in (a) shows the Fermi surface contour for Fermi energy EF = 0.1 eV with distribution of ⟨Sz⟩in units of ℏand the inset plot of (b) shows the Fermi surface contour for Fermi energy EF = 0.075 eV with distri- bution of ⟨Sz⟩. We consider ∆= 1 eV and γ = 0.25 eV with JA = 1 eV, λR = 0 eV in (a) and JA = 0 eV, λR = 0.1 eV in (b).

FIG. 2. Variation of orbital Hall (OH) conductivities with Fermi energy EF is shown in (a) and (b), respectively, for different values of altermagnetic strength JA and the onsite energy ∆. We consider λR = 0.
FIG. 3. Variation of spin Hall (SH) conductivities with Fermi energy EF is shown in (a) and (b), respectively, for different values of SOC λR and the on-site orbital energy ∆. We con- sider JA = 0.

FIG. 4. Variation of OH conductivity with EF for different values of temperature is shown in (a). The Orbital Nernst (ON) conductivity for different values of JA with respect to the Fermi energy is displayed in (b).

FIG. 5. Variation of SH conductivity with EF for different values of temperature is shown in (a). The spin Nernst (SN) conductivity for different values of λR with respect to the Fermi energy is displayed in (b).
